questions.question: What is a triangle?
questions.explanation: It is the simplest closed polygon.

questions.question: What is the sum of interior angles of a triangle?
questions.explanation: This is called the angle sum property of a triangle.

questions.question: Define an equilateral triangle.
questions.correct_answer: A triangle with all sides equal.
questions.explanation: All its angles are also equal.

questions.question: Define an isosceles triangle.
questions.correct_answer: A triangle with two equal sides.
questions.explanation: The angles opposite the equal sides are equal.

questions.question: What is a scalene triangle?
questions.correct_answer: A triangle with all sides unequal.

questions.question: What is a right-angled triangle?
questions.correct_answer: A triangle with one angle equal to 90°.
questions.explanation: The side opposite the right angle is the hypotenuse.

questions.question: State the triangle inequality theorem.
questions.correct_answer: Sum of any two sides of a triangle is greater than the third side.
questions.explanation: This condition must be satisfied to form a triangle.

questions.question: Can a triangle have two right angles?
questions.explanation: The sum of angles would exceed 180°.

questions.question: Which side is opposite the largest angle in a triangle?
How many sides does a triangle have?
questions.explanation: A triangle is defined by three sides.

notes.title: Angle Sum Property
notes.content: The sum of the three interior angles of any triangle is always 180°.

notes.title: Types of Triangles
notes.content: Triangles can be classified based on sides and angles.

notes.title: Triangle Inequality
notes.content: A triangle is possible only if the sum of any two sides is greater than the third side.

facts.fact: The hypotenuse is always the longest side in a right-angled triangle.
A triangle cannot have more than one right angle.
